deep_why.statement: PSG exists so hard-working SMBs — including the ones in my own family and community — finally see their effort compound into real growth, by closing the enterprise cost gap with AI-powered services.
layers: Watched them work hard but lose ground
Their effort finally compounds into real growth

values.meaning: Ship things that hold up. No slop, no half-done. Quality wins ties.
Measure by their results, not our outputs. Deliverables are means, outcomes are the end.
Stay lean. No VC, no gatekeepers. Keep PSG free; keep clients free.
Build assets that gain value over time. Prefer durable systems to one-shot wins.

elevator_pitch.pitch: AI services for local businesses — Phoenix Solutions Group delivers AI-powered marketing, automation, and ops to SMBs that can't afford enterprise teams or tools.

floors.2: Phoenix Solutions Group delivers AI-powered marketing, automation, and ops to local businesses that can't afford enterprise teams or tools.
floors.3: SMBs can't hire five specialists or buy twelve SaaS tools. PSG bundles marketing, automation, content, and ops into one AI-powered service. Effort finally compounds into growth.
floors.4: Problem: hard-working SMBs lose ground because enterprise-grade marketing, automation, and ops are priced out of reach — meanwhile chains and big tech keep widening the gap. Insight: AI flips the cost equation; bundled into a productized service, it gives a five-person shop the leverage of a thirty-person team. Ask: PSG works with SMBs who want outcomes over deliverables, and partners who share the thesis that main street should keep its independence while growing.

surface_vision.summary: PSG works when clients can't help but tell us — unprompted texts like "booked solid again this month, thanks" — because the outcomes speak before the invoice does.
scenes: Client SMS: 'booked solid again this month, thanks' — unprompted, recurring, no nudge needed
